task_completion.description: The agent has opened the Google Earth application or website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Google Earth interface (globe or map view) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent has activated the measurement or ruler tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Measure' or 'Ruler' tool interface/dialog box is open, or the cursor has changed to a measurement crosshair.

task_completion.description: The starting point of the measurement is placed on the Statue of Liberty.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement pin or dot is clearly located on Liberty Island (the small island south of Manhattan) or directly on the Statue of Liberty.

task_completion.description: The ending point of the measurement is placed on the Empire State Building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement pin or dot is located in Midtown Manhattan at the location of the Empire State Building.

task_completion.description: The tool displays the calculated distance between the two points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distance value is visible in the measurement tool window (expected value approx 5.3-5.4 miles or 8.6-8.7 km).

integrity.description: The agent did not simply search for the distance using a search engine (e.g., Google Search text query).
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen should show the Google Earth map interface, not a Google Search results page with the answer in text.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical map interface to measure, not a terminal or code script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement line and pins are visible on the graphical map rendering, not just a text output in a console.

integrity.description: A visual line connects the two landmarks on the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: A yellow or white line (typical for Google Earth ruler) connects Liberty Island and Midtown Manhattan.
